feed.title: BatBato's Blog
feed.subtitle: A blog to take notes of what I learn, Writ-Up and many more...

entry.title: CTFs | CTF_INSA_2024 | Web | SQLi
entry.summary: SQLi 1 Here, we are tasked to perform SQL injections. An IPS is configured, this means that we can’t use tools like SQLMap because we would be blocked. We can see, that we are facing a login page. In CTF environment, the first thing that comes to your mind when you see a login page (after trying default credentials) is to perform an SQLi to bypass the authentication process. Using the payloa...

entry.title: CTFs | CTF_INSA_2024 | Web | MegaUpload
entry.summary: MegaUpload 1 - Bypass upload filter For this challenge, we have a website where we can upload files. But the upload is limited to image files (.jpg) and text files (.txt), other files return an error: Each uploaded files are in /uploads/ directory. We can see that if we run a Gobuster on the website. We need to bypass the filter extension, for that, we can try to upload a .htaccess file ...

entry.title: CTFs | CTF_INSA_2024 | Web | LFI
entry.summary: LFI 1 In this challenge, we are tasked to perform LFI on the given website. The LFI vulnerability often appear in the GET parameter directly in the URL. Here we can notice the ?page=accueil.php where the GET parameter is page and its value is accueil.php. Note that here we only have one GET parameter so the URL ends with ?page=... but if we would have multiple parameters, we would have a...

entry.title: CTFs | CTF_INSA_2024 | Web | Attention_au_swap
entry.summary: Attention au swap In this challenge, we are asked to demonstrate some kind of vulnerability regarding the text editor vim. The name of the challenge gives us a hint on where to look for (swap). First I searched swap vim and I found this link https://www.baeldung.com/linux/vim-swap-files. It explains what is a swap file and that if a file called {filename_whit_extention} is created then its s...

entry.title: CTFs | CTF_INSA_2024 | Systeme
entry.summary: Find You’re Path Here we are given username and password for the user bob. We can ssh into the machine as follows: As you can see, when we perform sudo -l we see that we can run the vim command as any user or group (ALL:ALL) without the need to specify our password. Lets have a look at how we can get a root shell with Vim on GTFOBins: We run the provided command and we have now a root sh...
